text: is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音慢速英语农业报道。

text: Growers around the world are using new methods to grow grapes to make wine.
zh: 世界各地的种植者都在使用新方法来种植酿酒葡萄。

text: These include natural and organic methods
zh: 这些方法包括天然和有机的方式，

text: to control harmful insects and weeds instead of using chemicals.
zh: 用来控制害虫和杂草，而不是使用化学品。

text: Now, a winery in Canada has imported a natural way to control its grapevines.
zh: 如今，加拿大的一家酒庄引入了一种天然的方式来管理葡萄藤。

text: The Featherstone Winery is in southern Ontario.
zh: 费瑟斯通酒庄位于安大略省南部。

text: It has eight hectares of perfect rows of grapevines.
zh: 那里有八公顷整齐排列的葡萄藤。

text: The vines, like other plants, need to be pruned every year.
zh: 和其他植物一样，葡萄藤每年都需要修剪。

text: In general, dead or living parts of plants
zh: 一般来说，植物上枯死或多余的部分

text: need to be removed to improve the shape or growth of the plant.
zh: 需要被剪除，以改善植株的形状或生长。

text: Pruning grapevines must be done very carefully.
zh: 修剪葡萄藤必须非常小心地进行。

text: Only a targeted area of leaves is removed from the lower part of the vines
zh: 只需去除葡萄藤下部特定区域的叶子，

text: to help the grapes grow better. But at the Featherstone Winery,
zh: 以帮助葡萄更好地生长。但在费瑟斯通酒庄，

text: no man or machine does the pruning.
zh: 修剪工作既不是人做的，也不是机器做的。

text: Instead, the job is done by 40 cute little woolly lambs.
zh: 而是由40只可爱的小绵羊来完成。

text: David Johnson owns the vineyard. He says he learned about using lambs
zh: 大卫·约翰逊是这家葡萄园的主人。他说他学会用绵羊做这件事，

text: while visiting wineries in New Zealand.
zh: 是在他访问新西兰酒庄期间。

text: Mr. Johnson says the young lambs are perfectly designed to do the job.
zh: 约翰逊先生说，这些小羊天生就适合做这份工作。

text: They eat the grape leaves on the lower parts of the vine,
zh: 它们会吃掉葡萄藤下部的叶子，

text: but they are not tall enough to reach the grapes.
zh: 但个头不够高，够不到葡萄。

text: They only weigh about 22 kilos, so they do not beat down the soil,
zh: 它们体重只有大约22公斤，所以不会把土壤踩实，

text: and their waste makes good organic fertilizer.
zh: 而且它们的粪便还是很好的有机肥料。

text: In addition, using the lambs costs much less than hiring workers
zh: 此外，使用绵羊的成本远低于雇人

text: to prune the vines for seven weeks in the summer.
zh: 在夏季花七周时间来修剪葡萄藤。

text: And when the pruning is done in August, the lambs,
zh: 而当八月修剪工作完成后，这些绵羊——

text: well you might not want to know this part, they become lamb chops. Tasty ones, says David Johnson.
zh: 你可能不太想知道接下来的事——就会变成羊排。大卫·约翰逊说，味道还很不错。

text: He says he had a difficult time finding enough lambs to to do the job.
zh: 他说自己费了不少功夫才找到足够多的羊来做这份工作。

text: There are about 50 million lambs in New Zealand, but there are not nearly as many in Ontario.
zh: 新西兰大约有五千万只绵羊，但安大略省的数量远没有那么多。

text: Also, even some organic pesticides are harmful to lambs,
zh: 而且，即便是一些有机农药，对绵羊也是有害的，

text: and the lambs must be supervised so they do not prune too much.
zh: 所以必须有人看管这些羊，以免它们啃得太过头。

text: David Johnson says the lambs carry out his environmental ideas about farming.
zh: 大卫·约翰逊说，这些绵羊正体现了他对环保农业的理念。

text: He says the lambs are lovely and peaceful and he likes having them in his vineyard.
zh: 他说这些羊既可爱又安静，他喜欢让它们待在自己的葡萄园里。

text: People visiting the vineyard also enjoy watching the lambs do their job.
zh: 前来参观葡萄园的游客也很喜欢观看这些羊工作。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: 以上就是美国之音慢速英语农业报道。

text: It was produced by Joyce Kryzak with support from the Park Foundation
zh: 本节目由乔伊斯·克里扎克制作，得到了帕克基金会

text: and the Gaylord and Dorothy Donnelly Foundation.
zh: 以及盖洛德与多萝西·唐纳利基金会的支持。

text: You can hear more stories and subscribe to the daily podcast
zh: 您可以收听更多相关报道，并订阅我们的每日播客，

text: at environmentreport .org.
zh: 网址是environmentreport.org。

text: And you can find more of our agriculture reports at voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: 您还可以在voaspecialenglish.com上找到更多我们的农业报道。
